title: Cannot read property of null/undefined
fixes: Add optional chaining (?.) before the property access
Add a null check before accessing the property
Ensure the variable is initialized before use
Check if an async operation has completed before accessing the result

title: Value is not a function
fixes: Check that the variable holds a function and not undefined/null
Verify the import path and that the function is exported correctly
Ensure you're not shadowing the function with a variable of the same name
Check for circular dependencies that may cause the import to be undefined

title: Assignment to constant variable
fixes: Change `const` to `let` if the variable needs reassignment
Use a new variable instead of reassigning the constant
For objects/arrays declared with const, mutate properties instead of reassigning

title: Value is not iterable
fixes: Ensure the value is an array or iterable before using for...of or spread
Provide a default empty array: `value || []`
Check that the API response returns an array, not an object or null
Use Array.from() to convert array-like objects to arrays

title: Cannot set property of null/undefined
fixes: Initialize the object before setting properties on it
Add a null check before the assignment
Ensure DOM element exists before setting attributes (check querySelector result)

title: Cannot convert undefined/null to object
fixes: Provide a fallback value: `Object.keys(value || {})`
Check for null/undefined before passing to Object.keys, Object.assign, etc.
Ensure the data source returns an object, not null

title: Variable is not defined
fixes: Import or declare the variable before using it
Check for typos in the variable name
For browser globals (window, document), ensure code runs in the correct environment
Add the missing dependency to package.json and install it

title: Cannot access variable before initialization (TDZ)
fixes: Move the variable declaration before its first usage
Check for circular dependencies between modules
Replace `let`/`const` with function declarations if hoisting is needed

title: Unexpected token
fixes: Check for missing commas, brackets, or parentheses near the reported line
Ensure JSON files don't have trailing commas or comments
Verify the file is being parsed with the correct module type (ESM vs CJS)
Check that optional chaining/nullish coalescing is supported by your target

title: Invalid regular expression
fixes: Escape special regex characters: . * + ? ^ $ { } ( ) | [ ] \
When using `new RegExp()`, double-escape backslashes: `\\d` instead of `\d`
Check for unbalanced parentheses or brackets in the pattern

title: JSON parse error
fixes: Validate the JSON string with a linter or JSON.parse in try/catch
Check for trailing commas, single quotes, or unquoted keys
Ensure the response is actually JSON and not HTML (check Content-Type header)
Look for control characters or BOM in the input string

regex: SyntaxError:.*Unexpected.*'export'|Cannot use import statement outside a module
title: Cannot use import/export outside a module
fixes: Add `"type": "module"` to package.json for ESM
Rename file to .mjs for ESM or .cjs for CommonJS
Configure your bundler/transpiler to handle ESM syntax
Use `require()` instead if targeting CommonJS

title: Maximum call stack size exceeded (infinite recursion)
fixes: Add a base case to your recursive function to stop recursion
Check for circular references in objects being serialized (JSON.stringify)
Convert recursion to iteration using a stack/queue
Check for infinite component re-renders (React useEffect deps, Angular change detection)

title: Invalid array length
fixes: Ensure the array length is a non-negative integer
Check that the value passed to `new Array()` is not negative or NaN
Validate input before using it as an array size

title: Invalid Date
fixes: Validate the date string format before passing to `new Date()`
Use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for cross-browser compatibility
Check for null/undefined being passed as a date argument
Use a date library (date-fns, dayjs) for reliable parsing

regex: TS2322:.*Type '.*' is not assignable to type
title: TS2322: Type is not assignable
fixes: Fix the type mismatch — ensure the value matches the declared type
Add a type assertion if you're certain of the type: `value as MyType`
Widen the target type to accept the value (e.g., union type)
Check if null/undefined needs to be included in the type definition

regex: TS2339:.*Property '.*' does not exist on type
title: TS2339: Property does not exist on type
fixes: Add the property to the interface/type definition
Use optional chaining if the property may not exist: `obj?.prop`
Cast to a more specific type that includes the property
Check for typos in the property name

regex: TS2345:.*Argument of type '.*' is not assignable to parameter of type
title: TS2345: Argument type mismatch
fixes: Ensure the argument matches the expected parameter type
Add a type assertion: `value as ExpectedType`
Update the function signature to accept the broader type
Transform the data to match the expected shape before passing

title: TS2532: Object is possibly undefined
fixes: Add a null/undefined check before accessing the object
Use optional chaining: `obj?.property`
Use non-null assertion if you're certain it's defined: `obj!.property`
Provide a default value with nullish coalescing: `obj ?? defaultValue`

title: TS2554: Wrong number of arguments
fixes: Pass the correct number of arguments to the function
Make parameters optional with `?` if they're not always needed
Add default values to parameters: `param = defaultValue`
Check the function signature — it may have changed

title: TS2769: No overload matches this call
fixes: Check which overload signatures exist and match your arguments to one
Cast arguments to the expected types for the correct overload
Ensure you're not passing extra or incompatible arguments
Check the library's type definitions for the correct usage

title: TS7006: Parameter implicitly has any type
fixes: Add an explicit type annotation to the parameter
Set `"noImplicitAny": false` in tsconfig.json (not recommended)
Define an interface for the parameter shape

title: TS18046: Value is of type unknown
fixes: Narrow the type with a type guard: `if (value instanceof Error)`
Use a type assertion after validation: `(value as MyType)`
Add a typeof check: `if (typeof value === 'string')`

regex: TS2307:.*Cannot find module '.*' or its corresponding type declarations
title: TS2307: Cannot find module or type declarations
fixes: Install the package: `npm install <package>`
Install type declarations: `npm install -D @types/<package>`
Create a declaration file (.d.ts) with `declare module '<package>'`
Check `tsconfig.json` paths and baseUrl configuration

title: TS2531: Object is possibly null
fixes: Add a null check: `if (obj !== null)`
Use non-null assertion operator: `obj!` (only when certain)
Use optional chaining: `obj?.method()`
Provide a fallback with nullish coalescing: `obj ?? default`

regex: TS2355:.*A function whose declared type is neither 'void' nor 'any' must return a value
title: TS2355: Function must return a value
fixes: Add a return statement for all code paths
Change the return type to include `undefined` or `void` if appropriate
Add return statements to else branches and after switch cases

regex: TS2740:.*Type '.*' is missing the following properties from type
title: TS2740: Type is missing required properties
fixes: Add the missing properties to the object literal
Make the properties optional in the interface with `?`
Use `Partial<Type>` if all properties should be optional
Check if you're using the correct interface for the context

title: Module not found
fixes: Run `npm install` to install missing dependencies
Check the import path for typos and correct casing
Verify the package is listed in package.json dependencies
Check that the module's `main`/`exports` field points to the correct file

title: Cannot require() an ES Module
fixes: Use dynamic `import()` instead of `require()`
Downgrade to an older version of the package that supports CJS
Convert your project to ESM by adding `"type": "module"` to package.json
Use a bundler that handles ESM/CJS interop

title: Unhandled promise rejection
fixes: Add `.catch()` to the promise chain
Wrap async code in try/catch
Add a global handler: `process.on('unhandledRejection', handler)`
Check that all async functions are awaited

regex: TypeError:.*\.then is not a function|is not a (thenable|promise)
title: Value is not a promise/thenable
fixes: Ensure the function returns a Promise (add `async` or return `new Promise()`)
Check that you're not calling .then() on a synchronous return value
Wrap the value in `Promise.resolve(value)` if needed

title: document is not defined (SSR/Node)
fixes: Guard DOM access with `if (typeof document !== 'undefined')`
Move DOM manipulation to lifecycle hooks that only run in the browser (ngAfterViewInit, useEffect)
Use `isPlatformBrowser()` in Angular or equivalent framework check
Use JSDOM or happy-dom for testing environments

title: window is not defined (SSR/Node)
fixes: Guard with `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`
Use dependency injection to abstract window access
Move browser-only code to client-side lifecycle hooks
Mock window in tests: `global.window = {}`

title: ENOENT: File or directory not found
fixes: Check the file path for typos and ensure it exists
Use path.resolve() or path.join() for cross-platform paths
Create the directory first with `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })`
Check that the working directory is correct

title: EACCES: Permission denied
fixes: Fix folder permissions: `chmod -R 755 <directory>` or take ownership on Windows
Avoid running npm with sudo — fix npm prefix: `npm config set prefix ~/.npm-global`
Run the terminal as Administrator on Windows
Check if the file is locked by another process

title: EPERM: Operation not permitted
fixes: Close applications that may have the file open (editors, dev servers)
Run terminal as Administrator (Windows) or with sudo (Unix)
Delete node_modules and reinstall: `rm -rf node_modules && npm install`
Disable antivirus temporarily if it's blocking file operations

title: EADDRINUSE: Port already in use
fixes: Kill the process using the port: `npx kill-port <port>` or `lsof -ti:<port> | xargs kill`
Use a different port via environment variable or CLI flag
On Windows: `netstat -ano | findstr :<port>` then `taskkill /PID <pid> /F`

regex: Error:.*ENOSPC|no space left on device|System limit for number of file watchers reached
title: ENOSPC: Too many file watchers or no disk space
fixes: Increase file watcher limit: `echo fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288 | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf && sudo sysctl -p`
Free disk space — clear node_modules caches: `npm cache clean --force`
Exclude node_modules from file watching in your dev tool config

regex: Module build failed.*loader|Module parse failed|You may need an appropriate loader
title: Webpack: Module parse/build failed — missing loader
fixes: Install the required loader (e.g., `npm install -D css-loader style-loader`)
Add a rule in webpack.config.js for the file type
Check that the file extension matches a configured module rule
Ensure the loader version is compatible with your webpack version

regex: error TS6059:.*is not under 'rootDir'|TS6305:.*Output file.*has not been built from source
title: TypeScript rootDir/project reference error
fixes: Ensure all source files are under the `rootDir` specified in tsconfig.json
Update `include`/`exclude` patterns in tsconfig.json
Build referenced projects first with `tsc --build`
Move the file under the correct source directory

title: Vite: Failed to resolve import
fixes: Install the missing dependency: `npm install <package>`
Add the path alias to `resolve.alias` in vite.config.ts
Check that the file extension is included if non-standard
Ensure the package has an ESM export in its package.json `exports` field

title: esbuild build failure
fixes: Check the specific error code and file/line reference in the output
Ensure syntax is compatible with the `target` setting in esbuild config
Add problematic packages to the `external` list if they shouldn't be bundled
Install missing dependencies referenced in the error

regex: Chunk \w+ \[\w+\] exceeds.*limit|entrypoint size limit|asset size limit
title: Webpack/build: Bundle size exceeds limit
fixes: Enable code splitting with dynamic imports: `import('./module')`
Analyze the bundle with `webpack-bundle-analyzer` to find large dependencies
Use tree-shaking-friendly imports: `import { x } from 'lib'` instead of `import lib`
Increase the limit in config or set `performance.hints` to 'warning'

title: Array method called on non-array
fixes: Ensure the value is an array before calling array methods
Provide a default empty array: `(data || []).map(...)`
For reduce on empty array, always provide an initial value as second argument
Check the API response shape — it may wrap the array in an object

title: Modern method not available (polyfill needed)
fixes: Check your Node.js version — upgrade to a version supporting the method
Add a polyfill via core-js: `import 'core-js/features/array/flat'`
Update the `target`/`lib` in tsconfig.json to include ES2019+
Use an alternative: `[].concat(...arr)` instead of `arr.flat()`

title: TS2304: Cannot find name
fixes: Import the type/variable from its module
Install type declarations: `npm install -D @types/node` or `@types/<lib>`
Add to `compilerOptions.types` in tsconfig.json
Declare the global in a .d.ts file: `declare const myGlobal: MyType`

regex: TS2688:.*Cannot find type definition file for
title: TS2688: Cannot find type definition file
fixes: Install the missing @types package: `npm install -D @types/<name>`
Remove the package from `types` array in tsconfig.json if not needed
Check that node_modules/@types/<name> exists and is not corrupted
Run `npm install` to restore missing packages

title: Circular dependency detected
fixes: Extract shared code into a third module that both modules can import
Use dependency injection or inversion of control to break the cycle
Move the import inside the function that needs it (lazy import)
Refactor barrel files (index.ts) — they often create accidental cycles

title: ENAMETOOLONG: File path too long
fixes: Shorten the file path or move the project closer to the root directory
On Windows, enable long paths: `git config --system core.longpaths true`
Flatten deeply nested node_modules with `npm dedupe`
Use pnpm or Yarn PnP which handle deep paths better

regex: heap out of memory|FATAL ERROR:.*allocation failed|JavaScript heap out of memory
title: JavaScript heap out of memory
fixes: Increase Node.js memory: `node --max-old-space-size=8192`
Set via env: `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`
Find and fix memory leaks — check for unbounded caches, growing arrays, or event listeners
Process large data in streams/chunks instead of loading everything into memory
